type.description: Type name used to identify the type

icon.description: Icon of the type, in base64

group.description: Group name used for grouping "similar" types

extra.description: Extra type info. Depends on specific type.

host.description: Host.Active Directory Server IP or Hostname

compat.description: Compatibility.Compatibility of AD connection (Usually windows 2000 and later)

username.description: User.Username with read privileges on the base selected (use USER@DOMAIN.DOM form for this)

password.description: Password.Password of the ldap user

search_base.description: Override Base.If not empty, will override AD search base with this value (format: dc=..., dc=...)

default_domain.description: Default domain.Domain used for users without domain (if empty, will use the domain of the user) if compat mode is not "Windows NT"

backup_servers_list.description: Backup servers.If not empty, this list of domain=backup1,backup2,... will be used as backup servers for the domains.If domain is not specified, the backup servers will be used for all domains.

timeout.description: Timeout.Timeout in seconds for network connections

use_ssl.description: Use SSL.If checked,  a ssl connection to Active Directory will be used

verify_ssl.description: Verify SSL.If checked, SSL verification will be enforced. If not, SSL verification will be disabled

certificate.description: Certificate.Certificate to use for SSL verification

mfa_attribute.description: MFA attribute.Attribute from where to extract the MFA code

host_translations.description: Host translation list.If not empty, this list of key=value pairs will be used to translate the host name from AD to an resolvable hostname.

ignore_domains.description: Ignore domains.If not empty, this list of domains will be ignored when searching for users. Comma separated values.

redirect_url.description: Redirect On Expired.If not empty, if user password is expired, will be automatically redirected to this URL

force_group_membership.description: Force Group Membership.If not empty, all users will be considered members of this group

ActiveDirectoryAuthenticator.description:
This class represents an Active Directory authenticator.
Delegates the actual AD communication to ``ad.AD``.

tenant_id.description: Tenant ID.Obtained from Entra ID - Properties - Tenant ID

client_id.description: Client ID.Obtained from App created on Azure for UDS Enterprise

client_secret.description: Client Secret.Obtained from App created on Azure for UDS Enteprise - Keys

callback.description: Callback.Callback url (Filled by UDS, fix this only if necesary!!)

https_proxy.description: Proxy.Proxy used for connection to azure for HTTPS connections (use PROTOCOL://host:port, i.e. https://10.10.0.1:8080)

use_msds.description: Enable School Data Sync Integration.Enable only if you have Microsoft School Data Sync in your Environnment.

logout_on_azure.description: Azure Logout method..UDS Redirection to Microsoft logout pages on UDS logout.

external_domain.description: External Domain (#EXT# users domain).Domain used for external users (empty means no external users)

allowed_tenants.description: Allowed Tenants.Comma-separated tenant IDs. Required to search or add users/groups of those tenants.

AzureADAuthenticator.description: This class represents the Azure Authenticator

port.description: Port.Ldap port (usually 389 for non ssl and 636 for ssl)

ssl.description: Use SSL.If checked, the connection will be ssl, using port 636 instead of 389

username.description: User.Username with read privileges on the eDirectory

password.description: Password.Password of the ldap user

timeout.description: Timeout.Timeout in seconds of connection to LDAP

verify_ssl.description: Verify SSL.If checked, SSL verification will be enforced. If not, SSL verification will be disabled

certificate.description: Certificate.Certificate to use for SSL verification

mfa_attr.description: MFA attribute.Attribute from where to extract the MFA code

EDirectoryAuthenticator.description:
This class represents the eDirectory authenticator.
It uses the LDAP Access to eDirectory to manage communication.

accepts_proxy.description: Accept proxy.If checked, requests via proxy will get FORWARDED ip address (take care with this bein checked, can take internal IP addresses from internet)

allowed_in_networks.description: Allowed only from this networks.This authenticator will be allowed only from these networks. Leave empty to allow all networks

unique_by_host.description: Different user for each host.If checked, each host will have a different user name

reverse_dns.description: Reverse DNS.If checked, the host will be reversed dns

accepts_proxy.description: Accept proxy.If checked, requests via proxy will get FORWARDED ip address (take care with this bein checked, can take internal IP addresses from internet)

common_groups.description: Common Groups.User will be assigned to this groups once authenticated. Comma separated list of groups

redirection_endpoint.description: Redirection endpoint.Redirection endpoint for OAuth2.  (Filled by UDS)

token_endpoint.description: Token endpoint.Token endpoint for OAuth2. Only required for "code" response type.

info_endpoint.description: User information endpoint.User information endpoint for OAuth2. Only required for "code" response type.

logout_url.description: Logout URL.URL to logout from OAuth2 provider. Allows {token} placeholder.

username_attr.description: User Name Attr.Attributes that contains the user name attributes or attribute patterns (one for each line)

groupname_attr.description: Group Name Attr.Attribute that contains the group name attributes or attribute patterns (one for each line)

realname_attr.description: Real Name Attr.Attribute that contains the real name attributes or attribute patterns (one for each line)

OAuth2Authenticator.description: This class represents an OAuth2 Authenticator.

nas_identifier.description: NAS Identifier.NAS Identifier for Radius Server

app_class_prefix.description: App Prefix for Class Attributes.Application prefix for filtering groups from "Class" attribute

global_group.description: Global group.If set, this value will be added as group for all radius users

use_message_authenticator.description: Use Message Authenticator.Use Message Authenticator for authentication

mfa_attr.description: MFA attribute.Attribute from where to extract the MFA code

SampleAuthenticator.description:
This class represents a sample authenticator.

As this, it will provide:
   * The authenticator functionality
      * 3 Groups, "Mortals", "Gods" and "Daemons", just random group names selected.. :-),
        plus groups that we enter at Authenticator form, from admin interface.
      * Search of groups (inside the 3 groups used in this sample plus entered)
      * Search for people (will return the search string + 000...999 as usernames)
   * The Required form description for administration interface, so admins can create
     new authenticators of this kind.

In this sample, we will provide a simple standard auth, with owner drawn
login form that will simply show users that has been created and allow web user
to select one of them.

For this class to get visible at administration client as a authenticator type,
we MUST register it at package __init__

:note: At class level, the translations must be simply marked as so
using gettext_noop. This is done in this way because we will translate
the string when it is sent to the administration client.

smsHttpMFA.description:
Simple explnation of this class:

Basically, this is an interface to sending SMS messages to a phone number with the code to be used in the MFA process.
The Code will be check by UDS, and is generated also by UDS.

Basically, we will describe the HTTP Requeset needed to send the SMS, and the response we expect to get.

Example, we have a service, that provides us a method of sending an SMS with the following:
* URL: https://myserver.com/sendsms
* Method: POST
* content-type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded
* Parameters: phone={phone}&message={message}
* Headers:
  - Authorization header: "Auth: 1234567890"
  - Content-Type header: "application/x-www-form-urlencoded"
  - NonsenseExtraHeader: "This is a nonsense example header"

If sms is sent, the response will be:
* Status code: 200
* Content-Type: application/json
* Content: {"status": "ok"}

The fields will have the following values:

url_pattern: https://myserver.com/sendsms
ignore_certificate_errors: False  (check certificate errors)
http_method: 1 (POST)
headers_parameters:
   Auth: 1234567890
   Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded
   NonsenseExtraHeader: This is a nonsense example header
parameters: phone={phone}&message={message}
encoding: 0 (UTF-8)
auth_method: 0 (No authentication, already done in the headers field)
auth_user_or_token: (empty)
auth_password: (empty)
valid_response_re: {"status": "ok"}  (This is a regex. Only 2xx,3xx responses will be considered ok)
response_error_action: 1 (Deny login)
login_without_mfa_policy: 1 (If MFA Field (in our example, phone) is not provided, deny login)

# following fields are not used in this example
login_without_mfa_policy_networks
allow_skip_mfa_from_networks

SampleProvider.description:
This class represents the sample services provider

In this class we provide:
   * The Provider functionality
   * The basic configuration parameters for the provider
   * The form fields needed by administrators to configure this provider

   :note: At class level, the translation must be simply marked as so
   using gettext_noop. This is so cause we will translate the string when
   sent to the administration client.

For this class to get visible at administration client as a provider type,
we MUST register it at package __init__.

SampleService1.description:
Basic service, the first part (variables) include the description of the service.

Remember to fill all variables needed, but at least you must define:
    * type_name
    * type_type
    * type_description
    * icon_file (defaults to service.png)
    * publication_type, type of publication in case it needs publication.
      If this is not provided, core will assume that the service do not
      needs publishing.
    * user_service_type, type of deployed user service. Do not forget this!!!

The rest of them can be ommited, but its recommended that you fill all
declarations shown in this sample (that in fact, are all)

This description informs the core what this service really provides,
and how this is done. Look at description of class variables for more
information.

sign_rdp_file.description: Sign RDP file.If checked, RDP file will be signed with the server configured in crypto manager.

TRDSTransport.description:
Provides access via RDS to service.
This transport can use an domain. If username processed by authenticator contains '@', it will split it and left-@-part will be username, and right domain

server_certificate.description: Certificate.Server certificate (public), can be found on your ovirt engine, probably at /etc/pki/ovirt-engine/certs/ca.der (Use the contents of this file).

fullscreen.description: Fullscreen Mode.If checked, viewer will be shown on fullscreen mode-

allow_usb_redirection.description: Enable USB.If checked, USB redirection will be allowed.

allow_usb_redirection_new_plugs.description: New USB Auto Sharing.Auto-redirect USB devices when plugged in.

allow_smartcards.description: Smartcard Redirect.If checked, SPICE protocol will allow smartcard redirection.

ssl_connection.description: SSL Connection.If checked, SPICE protocol will allow SSL connections.

overrided_proxy.description: Override Proxy.If not empty, this proxy will be used to connect to the service instead of the one provided by the hypervisor. Format: http://host:port

SPICETransport.description:

startup_time.description: Startup wait time.Time, in seconds, to wait for first tunnel connection to be established.

verify_certificate.description: Force SSL certificate verification.If enabled, the certificate of tunnel server will be verified (recommended).

force_new_window.description: Force new HTML Window.If checked, every connection will try to open its own window instead of reusing the "global" one.

TestTransport.description: Simpe testing transport. Currently a copy of URLCustomTransport

url_pattern.description: URL Pattern.URL Pattern to open (i.e. https://_IP_/test?user=_USER_

URLTransport.description:
Provides access via RDP to service.
This transport can use an domain. If username processed by authenticator contains '@', it will split it and left-@-part will be username, and right password

fixed_name.description: Username.If not empty, this username will be always used as credential

custom_cmd.description: vAPP.If UDS vAPP is selected as "Desktop", the FULL PATH of the app to be executed. If UDS vAPP is not selected, this field will be ignored.

sound.description: Enable sound.If checked, sound will be available

exports.description: Redirect home folder.If checked, user home folder will be redirected. (On linux, also redirects /media)

keyboard_layout.description: Keyboard.Keyboard layout (es, us, fr, ...). Empty value means autodetect.

quality.description: Quality.Quality value used on some pack formats.
